product: Okta Core management API + OAuth 2.0 for Okta (service app)

oauth_token_request.no_authorization_header: client auth is the signed assertion; private_key_jwt is the ONLY supported client auth method for OAuth-for-Okta service apps

form_fields.scope: space-separated okta.* scopes, e.g. okta.users.read okta.users.manage
form_fields.client_assertion: compact JWT signed by the app's private key

claims.sub: client_id (iss and sub must both equal the client_id)
claims.aud: the exact token endpoint URL, https://{org}/oauth2/v1/token
claims.exp: clock.now() + 300 (assertion lifetime pinned at 300s; docs cap it at one hour)

client_assertion_jwt.signing: signature = signer(ascii(header_b64url + '.' + claims_b64url)); the signer is injected (KMS-held key) and its raw bytes are base64url-encoded as the third segment

oauth_token_request.caching: reuse the token until 60 seconds before issued_at + expires_in (per the injected clock), then refresh before the next request

invalid_token_401.handling: invalidate the cached token, fetch a fresh one, retry the request exactly once; a second consecutive 401 raises an auth error naming the errorCode (never the token)

rate_limits.get_429: wait exactly (reset - response Date header) seconds via the injected wait function, then retry the SAME URL; initial attempt + 2 retries max, then RateLimitError carrying reset_epoch and the E0000047 errorCode
rate_limits.post_429: a create is non-idempotent: NEVER retried, no waiting — raise RateLimitError immediately with reset_epoch so the caller decides

429_body.errorSummary: API call exceeded rate limit due to too many requests.

pagination.rule: follow the rel=next URL verbatim — the mock plants a srvMarker param that a rebuilt URL loses; absence of a next link ends iteration
pagination.list_users: GET /api/v1/users?limit=200 returns an array of user objects with profile.login as the sync identity key

sync_semantics.create: POST /api/v1/users?activate=false with body {"profile": {...}} — activate defaults to true and must be overridden to stage users
sync_semantics.halt_on_create_429: report {created: [logins], existing: [logins], halted: {login, reset_epoch}} and stop issuing creates; halted is None on full success

mock_fixture.org_base_url: loopback http://127.0.0.1:{port} standing in for https://{yourOktaDomain}
mock_fixture.note: Date headers are served explicitly at fixed epochs so reset arithmetic is exact
